property_history.description: Property purchased for $625,000 - large 2000m² block with dwelling

property_history.title: Subdivided
property_history.description: Subdivision approved and registered - property split into two lots. Lot 1 (dwelling + 1000m²) retained as main residence. Lot 2 (1000m² vacant land) created for sale. Subdivision costs: $45,000

properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 36: Subdivision - Main Residence Retained (Lot 1)**

Large property subdivided. Lot 1 (dwelling) retained and sold - full main residence exemption.

**Timeline:**
- 10 May 2008 to 20 Oct 2024: Main residence (16.45 years)
- 15 Mar 2022: Subdivision registered (property split into 2 lots)
- Lot 1: Dwelling + 1000m² land (retained and sold)
- Lot 2: 1000m² vacant land (sold separately - see Lot 2)

**Key Rules:**
- Lot 1 contains dwelling and was main residence throughout
- Subdivision of main residence property
- Cost base allocation: 50% of original cost ($651,600 × 50% = $325,800)
- Plus 50% of subdivision costs ($45,000 × 50% = $22,500)
- **NO CGT PAYABLE** - Full main residence exemption for lot with dwelling

**Expected Result:**
- NO CGT PAYABLE - Lot 1 qualifies for full main residence exemption
- Total capital gain (before exemption): $1,189,300
- Full exemption applies

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-110 ITAA 1997 (Main Residence Exemption)
- TR 97/11 (Subdivision of Main Residence)
- Lot containing dwelling eligible for full exemption

properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 36: Subdivision - Vacant Land Sold (Lot 2)**

Vacant land created from subdivision of main residence - partial exemption applies.

**Timeline:**
- 15 Mar 2022: Lot created from subdivision (1000m² vacant land)
- 15 Mar 2022 to 10 Jul 2024: Vacant land held (2.32 years)
- Deemed acquisition date: 15 Mar 2022

**Key Calculations:**
- Allocated cost base: $325,800 (50% of original)
- Subdivision costs: $22,500 (50% of total)
- Total cost base: $348,300
- Sale price: $685,000
- Selling costs: $19,225
- Capital gain: $317,475
- Partial main residence exemption available (adjacent land rules)
- After partial exemption and 50% CGT discount
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $52,913**

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $52,913
- Partial exemption applies for period property was main residence (up to subdivision)

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- TR 97/11 (Subdivision of Main Residence)
- Section 118-120 ITAA 1997 (Adjacent Land)
- Partial exemption for period land was part of main residence
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount)

additional_info.expected_outcome: Lot 1: NO CGT PAYABLE (full exemption); Lot 2: NET CAPITAL GAIN = $52,913 (partial exemption)
